nodes.label: Design System Generation
nodes.rationale: UI work must begin with a comprehensive design-system search so pattern, style, color, typography, effects, reasoning, and anti-patterns are selected together before implementation.

nodes.label: Master and Page Override Pattern
nodes.rationale: Persisted page-specific rules override a global master design system, allowing local deviations without losing a shared source of truth.

nodes.label: Compressed Subagent Delegation
nodes.rationale: Caveman-style subagent output is preferred when concise tool results preserve main-context budget across many delegations.

nodes.label: Cavecrew Auto-Clarity
nodes.rationale: Subagents temporarily abandon compressed prose when security, irreversible actions, or ambiguity require normal English.

nodes.label: Commit Auto-Clarity
nodes.rationale: Breaking changes, security fixes, data migrations, and reverts always receive a body because future debuggers need context that a terse subject cannot carry.

nodes.label: Persistent Input Token Savings
nodes.rationale: Compressing memory files reduces recurring context cost because files such as CLAUDE.md are loaded at every session start.

nodes.label: Fixed-Argument Subprocess Fallback
nodes.rationale: The CLI fallback passes user content through stdin and uses a fixed argument list without shell interpolation, reducing command-injection risk.

nodes.label: Scoped File IO
nodes.rationale: The compressor reads and writes only the user-specified file and its adjacent backup, and rejects files larger than 500KB before API use.

nodes.label: Compression Preservation Contract
nodes.rationale: Technical substance, code blocks, inline code, paths, commands, links, identifiers, numeric values, and document structure must survive compression exactly.

nodes.label: Review Auto-Clarity
nodes.rationale: Security findings, architectural disagreements, and onboarding contexts use normal prose because the author needs explanation and rationale beyond a terse finding.

nodes.label: Session Log Token Measurement
nodes.rationale: Usage numbers come from the JSONL session log rather than model estimation so the receipt reflects actual input and output tokens.

nodes.label: Token-Efficient Prose
nodes.rationale: Dropping articles, filler, pleasantries, and hedging reduces output tokens while preserving technical details, code, errors, and symbols.

nodes.label: Caveman Auto-Clarity
nodes.rationale: Normal prose temporarily replaces compressed fragments when security, irreversible actions, ordered sequences, or technical ambiguity make terseness unsafe.
